title: Union-closed sets conjecture: the |universe| = 2 special case
informal_statement: The union-closed sets conjecture (Frankl's conjecture): for every finite union-closed family of sets A (other than {empty set}), there exists an element belonging to at least half of the sets in A. This remains open in general. This packet proves the special case where the ambient universe is Fin 2 (every set in A is a subset of a 2-element type) by brute-force decision procedure over the finitely many possible families A.
informal_proof_idea: Since Fin 2 is finite, Finset (Finset (Fin 2)) is itself a finite type with finitely many elements, so the whole statement (after introducing A and its two hypotheses) reduces to a decidable proposition that the kernel can check by exhaustive case analysis via `decide +revert +kernel`.
formal_statement_pp: theorem union_closed_univ_card_two : ∀ (A : Finset (Finset (Fin 2))), A ≠ {∅} → (∀ᵉ (X ∈ A) (Y ∈ A), X ∪ Y ∈ A) → ∃ i, (1 / 2 : ℚ) * (A.card : ℚ) ≤ ((A.filter (fun x => i ∈ x)).card : ℚ)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves ONLY the |universe|=2 special case of the union-closed sets conjecture (Frankl's conjecture); the general conjecture (arbitrary finite ambient type) remains open, untouched by this packet -- the upstream sibling file's general statement (`union_closed`) is `sorry`/`research open`. This is a RESTATEMENT, not a byte-for-byte transport: the upstream `IsUnionClosed A` is a transparent `abbrev` for `∀ᵉ (X ∈ A) (Y ∈ A), X ∪ Y ∈ A`, inlined directly into this packet's root statement since abbrevs are not separate declarations `SubmitModule` needs to transport; the upstream `#A` / set-builder-filter notation (`#{x ∈ A | i ∈ x}`) is spelled out as plain `.card`/`.filter` since both are `scoped` behind `open Finset` in the sibling file and this environment's `problem_create` registers statements without that open context. Companion packet in the same file/cluster: `frontier.formal_conjectures.union_closed_singleton_mem.v1` (a different special case, authored in an earlier cycle by a concurrent agent, episode `ff2e3924-a37c-44aa-9c0d-b60e3bf4e81f`); a third proof-complete special case in the same file (`union_closed.variants.sharpness`, ~45 lines, showing the 1/2 constant cannot be improved) remains flagged as a candidate for a future cycle -- see `SOLVED_QUEUE.md`. Transported essentially verbatim (`intro A hA hUC; decide +revert +kernel`), kernel_verified on the FIRST attempt (episode 62b2eaf2-50d7-47ca-96ce-4a180fcf2cb4). training.eligibility: quarantined per this session's conservative default; open_problem_related: true since the general conjecture remains open.
